title: Add smooth hover animations to Club Cards
body:
### Background
Currently, the Club Directory cards change color abruptly on hover. This can be improved to provide a better user experience.

### Task
Add smooth transition animations to the Club Cards when hovered.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Cards should smoothly translate upwards by `2px` on hover.
- [ ] Ensure the shadow smoothly transitions to `shadow-md`.
- [ ] Transition duration should be `300ms`.

### Hints
- Check `src/components/ClubCard.tsx`.
- Use Tailwind classes like `transition-all duration-300 hover:-translate-y-1 hover:shadow-md`.

title: Implement Skeleton Loader for Event Details Page
body:
### Background
When navigating to an Event Details page, the screen is blank while data fetches. This can be improved to provide a better user experience.

### Task
Implement a Skeleton loader that matches the layout of the Event Details page.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Create a `SkeletonEventDetails` component.
- [ ] Render it while the `isLoading` state from React Query is true.
- [ ] Ensure it works nicely on mobile devices.

### Hints
- Check `src/routes/events.$eventId.tsx`.
- Use the Radix UI or Tailwind `animate-pulse` utility.

title: Add 'Copy Link' button to Event Pages
body:
### Background
Users currently have to manually copy the URL from their browser to share an event. This can be improved to provide a better user experience.

### Task
Add a 'Share' or 'Copy Link' button to the Event Details page that copies the current URL to the clipboard.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Add a button with a Link icon.
- [ ] On click, copy `window.location.href` to the clipboard.
- [ ] Show a toast notification confirming the link was copied.

### Hints
- Use the `sonner` toast library for notifications.
- Use the `navigator.clipboard.writeText` API.

title: Make Navbar responsive and collapse into a Hamburger Menu on Mobile
body:
### Background
On very small screens, the navbar links can get squished or overflow. This can be improved to provide a better user experience.

### Task
Implement a responsive hamburger menu for mobile devices.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Hide standard navigation links on screens `< 768px`.
- [ ] Show a hamburger icon that opens a side drawer or dropdown with the links.
- [ ] Ensure the dropdown is accessible.

### Hints
- Check `src/components/Navbar.tsx`.
- You can use the Radix UI Dialog or Popover component for the mobile menu.

title: Add a Scroll to Top button on long pages
body:
### Background
For pages with long lists (like the events or discussions page), scrolling back to the top is tedious. This can be improved to provide a better user experience.

### Task
Add a floating 'Scroll to Top' button that appears when the user scrolls down past a certain threshold.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Button should be fixed at the bottom right of the screen.
- [ ] Button should only appear after scrolling down `300px`.
- [ ] Clicking it smoothly scrolls the window to the top.

### Hints
- You can implement this as a layout-level component in `src/routes/__root.tsx`.
- Use `window.scrollTo({ top: 0, behavior: 'smooth' })`.

title: Fix contrast ratio on disabled buttons in Dark Mode
body:
### Background
Currently, disabled buttons in dark mode have a text color that blends too much with the background, failing WCAG contrast guidelines. This can be improved to provide a better user experience.

### Task
Adjust the text and background color of disabled buttons in dark mode to ensure readability.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Disabled buttons must have a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1 against their background.
- [ ] Verify using Chrome DevTools accessibility inspector.

### Hints
- Check the CSS variables or Tailwind classes used for the `Button` component.
- Look in `src/components/ui/button.tsx`.

title: Add ARIA labels to Icon-only Buttons
body:
### Background
Several buttons (like the theme toggle, or social media links) only contain icons and lack text. This can be improved to provide a better user experience for screen reader users.

### Task
Add descriptive `aria-label` attributes to all icon-only buttons across the app.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The Theme Toggle button should have `aria-label="Toggle theme"`.
- [ ] Social links in the footer should have appropriate labels (e.g., `aria-label="GitHub Profile"`).
- [ ] Any other icon-only buttons should be addressed.

### Hints
- Do a global search for `<Button size="icon"` or `<a` tags that only contain an `<Icon />`.

title: Enable Keyboard Navigation for Dropdown Menus
body:
### Background
Users who rely on keyboards cannot easily navigate some of the custom dropdown menus. This can be improved to provide a better user experience.

### Task
Ensure that all dropdown menus can be opened, navigated, and closed using the keyboard (Tab, Enter, Escape, Arrow Keys).

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pressing `Enter` or `Space` opens the menu.
- [ ] `Arrow Down`/`Arrow Up` navigate through options.
- [ ] `Escape` closes the menu and returns focus to the trigger.

### Hints
- We use Radix UI, which handles most of this out-of-the-box. Check if any custom implementations broke this behavior.

title: Add focus rings to all interactive form elements
body:
### Background
Some form inputs lose their focus ring outline when clicked due to CSS resets. This can be improved to provide a better user experience.

### Task
Ensure all inputs, textareas, and selects display a clear focus ring when active.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Inputs should have a visible outline, e.g., `focus:ring-2 focus:ring-primary`.
- [ ] Check Login, Signup, and Create Event forms.

### Hints
- Look at the `Input` and `Textarea` components in `src/components/ui/`.

title: Implement 'Skip to Content' link for accessibility
body:
### Background
Screen reader users have to tab through the entire navbar on every page load. This can be improved to provide a better user experience.

### Task
Add a 'Skip to Content' link at the very top of the DOM that is visually hidden until focused via the keyboard.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Link should be the first focusable element on the page.
- [ ] It should visually appear only when it receives focus.
- [ ] Clicking it should jump focus to the `<main>` element.

### Hints
- Add this in `src/routes/__root.tsx`.
- Tailwind classes: `sr-only focus:not-sr-only focus:absolute focus:z-50 focus:p-4`.

title: Extract repetitive Event Date formatting into a utility function
body:
### Background
Currently, we use `date-fns` inline across multiple components to format event dates. This can be improved to provide a better developer experience.

### Task
Create a centralized utility function for formatting event dates and replace inline usages.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Create `formatEventDate(dateString)` in `src/lib/utils.ts`.
- [ ] Replace at least 3 inline instances of formatting with this function.
- [ ] Ensure the format remains consistent (e.g., `MMM do, yyyy - h:mm a`).

### Hints
- Check `EventCard.tsx` and `EventDetails.tsx`.

title: Consolidate Button variants in Tailwind config
body:
### Background
The Button component has a lot of scattered styles. This can be improved to provide a better developer experience.

### Task
Review the `buttonVariants` in `src/components/ui/button.tsx` and ensure they align perfectly with our global design system.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Ensure `primary`, `secondary`, `destructive`, and `ghost` variants are clearly defined.
- [ ] Remove any unused or duplicate variants.

### Hints
- Use `class-variance-authority` (cva) effectively.

title: Add Unit Tests for Date Utility Functions
body:
### Background
We rely heavily on date manipulation but lack automated tests to catch regressions. This can be improved to provide a better developer experience.

### Task
Write unit tests for the date formatting functions in `src/lib/utils.ts`.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Create `src/lib/utils.test.ts`.
- [ ] Write at least 3 test cases for date formatting (handling past, future, and invalid dates).
- [ ] Tests must pass when running `bun run test`.

### Hints
- We use Vitest for testing. Import `describe`, `it`, and `expect` from `vitest`.

title: Implement basic Playwright E2E test for Login flow
body:
### Background
The critical login flow needs end-to-end testing to prevent regressions during major refactors. This can be improved to provide a better developer experience.

### Task
Set up a simple E2E test using Playwright that verifies the Login page renders correctly.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Add `@playwright/test` to devDependencies.
- [ ] Create a test that navigates to `/login`.
- [ ] Verify that the Email and Password inputs are present on the screen.

### Hints
- You may need to create a `playwright.config.ts`.
- Check Playwright documentation for setup instructions.

title: Clean up unused CSS classes and unused imports
body:
### Background
Over time, several files have accumulated unused imports and legacy CSS classes. This can be improved to provide a better developer experience.

### Task
Perform a code sweep to remove unused imports across the `src/components` directory.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Ensure ESLint does not report any `no-unused-vars` warnings.
- [ ] Remove any obviously dead code or commented-out old implementations.

### Hints
- Run `bun run lint` to easily spot unused variables.

title: Add 'Sort by Date' feature to Event Directory
body:
### Background
Events are currently listed in the order they are fetched. This can be improved to provide a better user experience.

### Task
Add a dropdown that allows users to sort events by "Newest First" or "Oldest First".

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Add a Radix UI Select component to the Event Directory header.
- [ ] Implement the sorting logic locally on the fetched array.
- [ ] Ensure the selected sort state is preserved during session.

### Hints
- Check `src/routes/events.tsx`.
- You can use standard JavaScript `.sort()` on the array before rendering.

title: Implement 'Clear All Filters' button
body:
### Background
Users have to manually uncheck filters to see all results again. This can be improved to provide a better user experience.

### Task
Add a "Clear All" button next to the active filters.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Button should only appear if at least one filter is active.
- [ ] Clicking it resets the filter state to its default "All" state.

### Hints
- Check `src/routes/events.tsx` where the `filter` state is managed.
- Set `setFilter("All")` on click.

title: Add Pagination or Infinite Scroll to Discussions Feed
body:
### Background
If there are hundreds of discussion posts, loading them all at once will degrade performance. This can be improved to provide a better user experience.

### Task
Implement infinite scrolling or basic pagination for the discussions feed.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Fetch posts in chunks (e.g., 10 at a time).
- [ ] Add a "Load More" button at the bottom, OR automatically fetch more when scrolling to the bottom.
- [ ] Update the Supabase query to use `.range()`.

### Hints
- Look into React Query's `useInfiniteQuery`.

title: Implement 'Similar Events' section on Event Details page
body:
### Background
Users viewing an event might be interested in other events from the same club or category. This can be improved to provide a better user experience.

### Task
Display 3 related events at the bottom of the Event Details page.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Fetch 3 events that share the same category or club ID as the current event.
- [ ] Ensure the current event is NOT included in the similar events list.
- [ ] Display them using the standard `EventCard` component.

### Hints
- Write a new Supabase query in `src/routes/events.$eventId.tsx`.

title: Add confirmation modal before deleting a discussion post
body:
### Background
Currently, clicking 'Delete' on a post immediately removes it, leading to accidental deletions. This can be improved to provide a better user experience.

### Task
Implement a confirmation modal that appears before a delete action is executed.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Use a Radix UI `AlertDialog`.
- [ ] Ask the user "Are you sure you want to delete this post? This action cannot be undone."
- [ ] Only execute the Supabase delete query if they click "Confirm".

### Hints
- Look at `src/components/ui/alert-dialog.tsx` for the component.

title: Create a robust Database View for Analytics dashboard
body:
### Background
The analytics dashboard currently makes multiple complex queries which is slow. This can be improved to provide a better developer experience.

### Task
Create a PostgreSQL Database View in Supabase to pre-calculate RSVP counts and event metrics.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Write a SQL migration script to create a view `club_analytics_view`.
- [ ] The view should join the `events` and `rsvps` table to count attendees per event.
- [ ] Update the frontend to fetch from this view instead of calculating locally.

### Hints
- Use the Supabase SQL Editor to test your query first.

title: Enforce RLS (Row Level Security) on the User Profiles table
body:
### Background
User profile data needs to be secure so users can only update their own profiles. This can be improved to provide better security.

### Task
Ensure that the Supabase RLS policies are strictly configured for the `profiles` table.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Add an RLS policy that allows `SELECT` for all authenticated users.
- [ ] Add an RLS policy that allows `UPDATE` only if `auth.uid() = id`.
- [ ] Provide the SQL script used to create these policies in the PR.

### Hints
- Review Supabase Documentation on Row Level Security.

title: Implement Webhook for New Club Registrations
body:
### Background
Admins need to be notified when a new club applies for approval. This can be improved by adding a webhook.

### Task
Set up a Supabase Database Webhook that fires when a new row is inserted into the `clubs` table.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The webhook should trigger on `INSERT` to the `clubs` table.
- [ ] It should send a POST request to a mock endpoint (or Discord webhook if provided).
- [ ] Document the setup process in the PR.

### Hints
- This is entirely configured in the Supabase Dashboard under Database -> Webhooks.

title: Add Database Index on Event Start Dates
body:
### Background
As the number of events grows, querying for "upcoming events" will become slower. This can be improved to provide a better user experience.

### Task
Add a PostgreSQL B-Tree index on the `start_time` column of the `events` table.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Write the SQL migration to create the index.
- [ ] Verify query performance using `EXPLAIN ANALYZE` in Supabase.
- [ ] Provide the SQL script in the PR description.

### Hints
- SQL syntax: `CREATE INDEX idx_events_start_time ON events(start_time);`

title: Implement rate limiting for password reset requests
body:
### Background
Malicious users could spam the password reset endpoint, exhausting our email quota. This can be improved to provide better security.

### Task
Implement a basic rate limit for requesting password reset emails.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Track recent requests (either in a small database table or using Supabase edge functions).
- [ ] Limit users to 3 password reset requests per hour.
- [ ] Return a `429 Too Many Requests` error if exceeded.

### Hints
- This might require creating a custom Supabase Edge Function to handle the reset request securely.
